questions.trap_type: Using 360/12 = 30 degrees per hour but forgetting minute hand movement

questions.question: A watch which gains 5 seconds in 3 minutes was set right at 7 a.m. In the afternoon of the same day, when the watch indicated quarter past 4 o'clock, the true time is:
questions.explanation:
[TABLE]

3 min. 5 sec. of this clock = 3 min. of the correct clock.

[TABLE]

[TABLE]

 = 9 hrs of the correct clock.

 The correct time is 9 hrs after 7 a.m. i.e., 4 p.m.

questions.question: At what time between 7 and 8 o'clock will the hands of a clock be in the same straight line but, not together?
questions.explanation:
When the hands of the clock are in the same straight line but not together, they are 30 minute spaces apart.

At 7 o'clock, they are 25 min. spaces apart.

 Minute hand will have to gain only 5 min. spaces.

55 min. spaces are gained in 60 min.

[TABLE]

[TABLE]
questions.trap_type: Using 360/12 = 30 degrees per hour but forgetting minute hand movement

questions.question: At what time between 5.30 and 6 will the hands of a clock be at right angles?
questions.explanation:
At 5 o'clock, the hands are 25 min. spaces apart.

To be at right angles and that too between 5.30 and 6, the minute hand has to gain (25 + 15) = 40 min. spaces.

55 min. spaces are gained in 60 min.

[TABLE]

[TABLE]
questions.trap_type: Using 360/12 = 30 degrees per hour but forgetting minute hand movement
